title: ADHD Screening: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
ADHD, the report will include the type of ADHD (inattentive, hyperactive-impulsive, or combined). It will also say whether ADHD is mild, moderate, or severe. Any other disorders that may have been found will be included in the report.
There's no cure for ADHD, but treatment may help reduce symptoms. Treatment may include medicine, talk therapy, behavioral therapy, and lifestyle changes. Usually, two or more treatments are combined. Parents of children with ADHD may also receive training to help their children manage ADHD.
Learn more about laboratory tests, reference ranges, and understanding results .

Centers for Disease Control and Prevention [Internet]. Atlanta: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services; About Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD); [updated 2022 Aug 9; cited 2022 Dec 8]; [about 4 screens]. Available from: https://www.cdc.gov/adhd/about/ 
CHADD [Internet]. Lanham (MD): CHADD; c2
